methods.description: Chemical fixation, embedding, trimming, mounting, and coating of biological samples for Focused Ion Beam - Scanning Electron Microscopy (FIB-SEM) imaging.

steps.description: For FIB-SEM, upon high pressure freezing, the cocktail of the freeze substitution contained 2% (w/v) osmium tetroxide and 0.5% (w/v) uranyl acetate in dried acetone as in (9,17).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare freeze substitution cocktail for FIB-SEM sample fixation

steps.description: The cells were washed four times in anhydrous acetone for 15 min each at −30 °C
steps.searchable_summary: Wash fixed cells in anhydrous acetone

steps.description: and gradually embedded in anhydrous araldite resin.
steps.searchable_summary: Embed washed cells in anhydrous araldite resin

steps.description: The sample was trimmed with a 90° diamond knife (Diatome) to expose the cells at two surfaces (the imaging surface and the surface perpendicular to the focused ion beam, FIB) and optimize the acquisition (55). When targeting the symbiotic microalgae within its host, the trimming was targeted towards the periphery of the organisms where they were in higher numbers.
steps.searchable_summary: Trim embedded sample using a diamond knife to expose surfaces for FIB-SEM

steps.description: After the sample was trimmed, it was mounted onto the edge of a SEM stub (Agar Scientific) using silver conductive epoxy (CircuitWorks) with the trimmed surfaces facing up and towards the edge of the stub.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount trimmed sample onto a SEM stub using conductive epoxy

steps.description: The sample was gold sputter coated (Quorum Q150RS; 180 s at 30 mA)
steps.searchable_summary: Gold sputter coat the mounted sample

methods.description: Process for acquiring 3D image stacks by sequentially milling slices with a Focused Ion Beam and imaging the exposed surface with a Scanning Electron Microscope.

steps.description: and placed into the FIB-SEM for acquisition (Crossbeam 540, Carl Zeiss Microscopy GmbH).
steps.searchable_summary: Place gold-coated sample into FIB-SEM instrument

steps.description: Once the ROI was located in the sample, Atlas3D software (Fibics Inc. and Carl Zeiss Microscopy GmbH) was used to perform sample preparation and 3D acquisitions.
steps.searchable_summary: Locate Region of Interest (ROI) in the sample using Atlas3D software

steps.description: First, a 1 µm platinum protective coat (20-30 µm 2 depending on ROI) was deposited with a 1.5 nA FIB current.
steps.searchable_summary: Deposit platinum protective coat over the ROI using FIB
outputs: Sample with platinum coat over ROI

steps.description: The rough trench was then milled to expose the imaging cross-section with a 15 nA FIB current,
steps.searchable_summary: Mill a rough trench using FIB to expose the imaging cross-section

steps.description: followed by a polish at 7 nA.
steps.searchable_summary: Polish the rough trench using FIB

steps.description: The 3D acquisition milling was done with a 1.5 nA FIB current.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform sequential FIB milling for 3D acquisition

steps.description: For SEM imaging, the beam was operated at 1.5 kV/700 pA in analytic mode using an EsB detector (1.1 kV collector voltage) at a dwell time of 8 µs with no line averaging.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire SEM image of the exposed surface using specified parameters
inputs: SEM beam (1.5 kV/700 pA, analytic mode)
Imaging parameters (8 µs dwell, no line averaging)

steps.description: For each slice, a thickness of 8 nm was removed,
steps.searchable_summary: Remove a specific thickness of material using FIB after imaging

steps.description: and the SEM images were recorded with a pixel size of 8 nm, providing an isotropic voxel size of 8 × 8 × 8 nm 3 .
steps.searchable_summary: Record SEM image with specified pixel size

steps.description: Whole volumes were imaged with 1000-1200 frames, depending on the Brandtodinium cells.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire a stack of 1000-1200 sequential SEM images

steps.description: Raw FIB-SEM stacks are available at https://www.ebi.ac.uk/biostudies/studies/XXXXX. Source data are provided with this paper.
steps.searchable_summary: Access raw FIB-SEM data from online repository

methods.description: Software-based processing steps applied to acquired FIB-SEM image stacks, including registration, filtering, segmentation, 3D reconstruction, and morphometric analysis.

steps.description: The first step of image processing was to crop the free-living and symbiotic Brandtodinium cells using the open software Fiji (https://imagej.net/Fiji),
steps.searchable_summary: Crop specific cells from raw FIB-SEM image stacks using Fiji

steps.description: followed by image registration (stack alignment), Image registration was done by the FIJI plugin 'Linear Stack Alignment with SIFT'(56),
steps.searchable_summary: Perform image registration (stack alignment) on cropped stacks using Fiji SIFT plugin

steps.description: then fine-tuned by AMST (57).
steps.searchable_summary: Fine-tune image stack alignment using AMST software

steps.description: Aligned image stacks were filtered to remove noise and highlight contours using a Mean filter in Fiji (0.5 pixel radius).
steps.searchable_summary: Filter aligned image stacks using a Mean filter in Fiji

steps.description: Segmentation of organelles and other cellular compartments of Brandtodinium and Collodaria was carried out with 3D Slicer software (58) (www.slicer.org), using a manually-curated, semiautomatic pixel clustering mode (5 to 10 slices are segmented simultaneously in z) as in (17).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform semi-automatic segmentation of cellular compartments using 3D Slicer

steps.description: We assigned colours to segmented regions using paint tools and adjusted the threshold range for image intensity values.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign colors and adjust intensity thresholds for segmented regions in 3D Slicer

steps.description: Morphometric analyses were performed with the 3D slicer module "segmentStatistics" on the different segments (segmented organelles) and converted to µm 3 or µm 2 taking into account the voxel size of 8 nm (Table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate morphometric parameters (volume, surface area) from segmented regions using 3D Slicer
outputs: Morphometric measurements (volume, surface area) in µm3/µm2

methods.description: Acquisition of X-ray Fluorescence hyperspectral images using synchrotron radiation to determine elemental distribution at different resolutions.

steps.description: The emitted fluorescence signal was detected with energy-dispersive, large area (80 mm 2 ) SDD detectors equipped with a Be window (SGX from RaySpec).
steps.searchable_summary: Detect X-ray fluorescence signal using SDD detectors
inputs: SDD detectors (80 mm2, Be window, SGX)

steps.description: Images were acquired by raster-scanning the sample in the X-ray focal plane, with a 0.5×0.5 µm 2 step and 500 ms dwell time.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire XRF images by raster-scanning the sample at low resolution
inputs: Acquisition parameters (0.5x0.5 µm2 step, 500 ms dwell time)

steps.description: The detector response was calibrated over a thin film reference sample consisting of layers of elements in ng/mm 2 concentration sputtered on a 200 nm thick Si 3 N 4 membrane (RF7-200-S2371 from AXO), measured using the same acquisition parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate XRF detector response using a thin film reference sample (RF7-200-S2371)

steps.description: On ID16B-NA, a beam of 17.5 keV focused to 50×50 nm 2 through KB mirrors was used to excite the samples. The photon flux at sample was ~ 2·10 11 ph/s.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up X-ray beam parameters at beamline ID16B-NA for sample excitation
inputs: Beam parameters (17.5 keV, 50x50 nm2 focus, ~2e11 ph/s flux)

steps.description: High-resolution XRF images (50×50 nm 2 step size) were acquired in air, with a dwell time of 100 ms/pixel.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire high-resolution XRF images by raster-scanning
inputs: Acquisition parameters (50x50 nm2 step, 100 ms/pixel dwell time)

steps.description: Two 3-element SDD detector arrays were used to collect fluorescence from the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fluorescence signal using multiple SDD detector arrays

steps.description: The detector response was calibrated over a thin film reference sample (RF8-200-S2453 from AXO).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate XRF detector response using a thin film reference sample (RF8-200-S2453)

steps.description: High-resolution images were acquired for free and symbiotic microalgae, and in selected areas of the hosts.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire high-resolution XRF images for different biological sample types
outputs: High-resolution XRF hyperspectral images for different sample types

methods.description: Processing of XRF hyperspectral images to normalize data and calculate elemental mass fractions.

steps.description: Hyperspectral images acquired in both beamlines were normalized by the incoming photon flux
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize XRF hyperspectral images by incoming photon flux

steps.description: and subjected to the same data analysis protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply a standard data analysis protocol to normalized XRF images

steps.description: Elemental mass fractions were calculated from fundamental parameters with the PyMca software package (60), assuming a biological matrix
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate elemental mass fractions from processed XRF data using PyMca

methods.description: Acquisition of secondary ion images using NanoSIMS to determine isotopic composition and distribution at high spatial resolution.

steps.description: beam was focused to a nominal spot size of ~ 120-150 nm
steps.searchable_summary: Focus the primary ion beam for NanoSIMS acquisition
outputs: Focused primary ion beam (~120-150 nm spot size)

steps.description: and stepped over the sample in a 256×256 pixel raster to generate secondary ions with a dwelling time of 5 ms/pixel.
steps.searchable_summary: Raster scan the sample with the focused ion beam to generate secondary ions
inputs: Acquisition parameters (256x256 pixel raster, 5 ms/pixel dwell time)
outputs: Secondary ions generated from sample surface

steps.description: The raster areas (scanning surface area) were defined based on previous SEM/TEM observations and were between 10x10 µm 2 to 40×40 µm 2 .
steps.searchable_summary: Define raster areas for NanoSIMS acquisition based on prior microscopy
outputs: Defined raster areas (10x10 µm2 to 40x40 µm2)

steps.description: Secondary ions ( 19 F -, 12 C 2 -, 13 C 12 C -, 12 C 14 N -, 12 C 15 N -, 31 Pand 32 S -) were simultaneously collected in electron multipliers at a mass resolution (M/ΔM) of about 8000 (Cameca definition).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect specific secondary ions using electron multipliers and mass spectrometer
outputs: Secondary ion counts/spectra for specified isotopes

steps.description: Each NanoSIMS image consist of 6 to 10 sequential images,
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire a stack of 6 to 10 sequential NanoSIMS images for each raster area

steps.description: For control (i.e. not enriched) samples, four images were acquired including 5 symbiotic dinoflagellates and two different areas of the host cell (Table S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire NanoSIMS images for control samples (symbiotic dinoflagellates, host areas)

steps.description: For 13 C and 15 N-enriched samples, two distinct collodarian colonies were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire NanoSIMS images for 13C and 15N-enriched samples (collodarian colonies)

methods.description: Processing and analysis of NanoSIMS images to correct for drift, accumulate sequential frames, and quantify isotopic enrichments in sub-cellular structures.

steps.description: drift corrected, ... using the software Look@NanoSIMS (61)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform drift correction on sequential NanoSIMS images using Look@NanoSIMS

steps.description: and accumulated using the software Look@NanoSIMS (61)
steps.searchable_summary: Accumulate drift-corrected sequential images using Look@NanoSIMS

steps.description: to quantify mean 13 C and 15 N enrichments of different sub-cellular structures.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify mean 13C and 15N enrichments in sub-cellular structures from accumulated images using Look@NanoSIMS
outputs: Mean 13C and 15N enrichments per sub-cellular structure

steps.description: These control images were used to determine the standard ratio against which the experimental 13 C-and 15 N-enrichments were quantified.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine standard isotopic ratios from control NanoSIMS images

steps.description: For 13 C and 15 N-enriched samples, ... experimental 13 C-and 15 N-enrichments were quantified.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify experimental 13C and 15N enrichments relative to the standard ratio
